title: Touch of Time
summary: A desk-based productivity object that turns the familiar analog clock face into a tactile, low-friction timer.
role: Concept development, product design, interaction design, design storytelling and visual presentation

sections.title: Reframing the alarm clock for the desk
sections.body: Touch of Time started by questioning the long-used definition of an alarm clock. Rather than treating the clock only as a bedside wake-up device, the concept responds to small tasks, rough deadlines and timeboxing moments throughout a work or study day.

sections.title: The clock face becomes the interface
sections.body: Hour markings act as tactile timer buttons. A user can set a rough target such as ‘finish by 2:10’ without unlocking a phone, scrolling through numbers or calculating a countdown duration. Selected buttons illuminate to give immediate visual feedback.
sections.imageAlt: Touch of Time clock-face interaction concept
sections.caption: A familiar visual language carries a new physical interaction.

items.title: Familiar surface
items.text: The analog face remains readable while becoming the main control surface.

items.title: Natural time setting
items.text: Task timing follows how people refer to points in the day, rather than requiring exact countdown maths.

items.title: Desk first
items.text: The object supports quick planning without introducing phone-based distraction.

sections.title: Form and behaviour work together
sections.body: The interaction is placed on the front of the product instead of hiding controls on the back. Button placement, tactile input and illuminated feedback connect the product’s physical form with its timing behaviour.
sections.caption: Physical controls are integrated into the face.

sections.title: A clock series for modern work routines
sections.body: The final concept combines traditional clock reading with tactile timer-setting interaction, adapting a familiar object to fragmented desk-based tasks.

images.alt: Final Touch of Time desk clock concept

sections.title: Beyond redesigning form
sections.body: This project explores how industrial design can challenge an existing product definition. It also strengthened my interest in interaction design, where physical interaction, product behaviour and everyday routines connect.

sections.title: Project in brief
sections.body: Individual concept project covering product definition, interaction logic, industrial design and visual storytelling.
